code

Open Source Monetization: Credit-Based Billing for CLI Tools

Published March 21, 2026 · 9 min read

The Open Source Monetization Problem

You've built a beloved open-source CLI tool. Thousands of stars on GitHub. Active community. But you're not making money.

Traditional options are terrible:

  • Patreon/Open Collective: Gets you $500-2K/month if you're lucky. Doesn't scale.
  • SaaS conversion: Removes the open-source advantage. Users abandon you for free forks.
  • Sponsorships: Feels like selling out. Requires constant pitching.
  • Support contracts: High friction. Only works if you have enterprise users.

The solution? Credit-based billing. Keep your CLI open-source and free, but charge for AI-powered features or high-volume usage.

The MCU Credit Model

MCU = Mission Completion Unit. One credit equals one meaningful task completion (writing, coding, analysis).

How It Works

User runs CLI command
         ↓
System checks: Is this feature AI-powered?
         ↓
If YES:
  - Check user's MCU balance
  - If balance greater than 0: Execute task, deduct 1 MCU
  - If balance equals 0: Show insufficient credits message
         ↓
If NO:
  - Execute freely (no cost)

Examples of free vs. paid:

Feature Cost Why
mekong list - List files Free Local filesystem operation
mekong cook - AI mission 1-5 MCU LLM inference costs money
mekong fix - Debug issue 2-3 MCU Requires analysis + generation
mekong deploy - Deploy code Free Just orchestrates your infrastructure
mekong review - Code review 3 MCU Analysis-heavy, requires LLM

Key insight: Users don't pay for the CLI. They pay for the AI computations it enables.

Monetization Architecture (4 Revenue Streams)

Stream 1: MCU Credit Subscriptions (Recurring)

Users subscribe to monthly credit allowances via NOWPayments webhooks.

// Pricing tiers (NOWPayments)
Starter: $49/mo → 200 MCU credits
  - Good for: Side projects, hobby CLI usage

Pro: $149/mo → 1,000 MCU credits
  - Good for: Teams, production tools

Enterprise: $499/mo → Unlimited MCU credits
  - Good for: Large teams, commercial use

Revenue model: 80% of revenue (assuming 200 users on Pro at $149/mo = $29,800 MRR)

Stream 2: Pay-As-You-Go (Variable)

Users without subscriptions can buy credits à la carte via Stripe.

// Stripe credit packs
10 MCU credits: $5 (50 cents per MCU)
50 MCU credits: $20 (40 cents per MCU)
100 MCU credits: $35 (35 cents per MCU)

Revenue model: 10% of revenue (impulse buyers, infrequent usage)

Stream 3: Seat Licenses (Enterprise)

Team of 5+ users? Sell per-seat annual licenses for commercial use.

// Annual licenses (via NOWPayments)
Team License: $299/year per seat
  - 5 seats = $1,495/year
  - Features: Shared credit pool, team management

Business License: $699/year per seat
  - For companies over 100 employees
  - Features: All Team + dedicated support

Revenue model: 10% of revenue (converts 5-10% of user base to team licenses)

Stream 4: API Tier (Programmatic Access)

Developers building on top of your CLI pay per API call.

// HTTP API (for embedding Mekong in other tools)
POST https://api.agencyos.network/v1/missions
Authorization: Bearer sk_live_...
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "command": "mekong cook 'write marketing email'",
  "user_id": "user_123"
}

// API pricing (pay-as-you-go only)
$0.50 per API call (1 MCU equivalent)
No free tier, starts with $50 prepaid credits

Revenue model: Negligible initially, but upside as ecosystem grows

Financial Projections

Assumptions: 1,000 total users at steady state

Segment Users MRR/User Total MRR
Free tier (10 MCU) 700 $0 $0
Pay-as-you-go (occasional) 150 $5 $750
Starter ($49) 80 $49 $3,920
Pro ($149) 50 $149 $7,450
Enterprise ($499) 20 $499 $9,980
TOTAL MRR $22,100

At 1,000 users: $22,100 MRR = $265K ARR (before payment processor fees)

Path to $1M ARR:

  • Reach 4,500 users (10x growth)
  • Convert 10% to paid (instead of 2% currently)
  • Increase average LTV through upsells

Key Learnings

  • Free tier is essential: 70% of users will try free. Remove friction.
  • Freemium works: Credit model converts 2-5% of free users to paid. That's your TAM.
  • Subscription wins: $149/mo users generate 30x more ARR than $5 one-time buyers.
  • NOWPayments is simple: Webhooks handle subscriptions automatically. No billing infrastructure needed.
  • HTTP 402 is underused: Most apps ignore it. Use it clearly for payment-required errors.
  • Transparency wins: Show cost before execution. Users will upgrade if the value is clear.

How to Start

1. Add credit tracking to your database

CREATE TABLE users (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  email TEXT UNIQUE,
  mcu_balance INT DEFAULT 10,
  subscription_tier TEXT,
  created_at DATETIME
);

CREATE TABLE transactions (
  id TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
  user_id TEXT,
  action TEXT,
  amount_mcu INT,
  source TEXT,
  timestamp DATETIME
);

2. Add middleware for credit checking

Implement a middleware that checks user balance before executing paid features.

3. Set up NOWPayments webhooks

Listen for subscription.created, subscription.updated, and subscription.cancelled events.

4. Update your CLI

Show balance before execution. Return HTTP 402 when credits run out.

5. Launch

mekong deploy --platform cloudflare
# Your API is live on workers.dev

# Update CLI to point to your API
export LLM_API_BASE=https://your-api.cloudflare.dev

# Announce the feature
# "Now monetized! Free tier for everyone."

Fork & Deploy Your Own

The full Mekong RaaS billing architecture is open-source. Deploy in 30 minutes.

View on GitHub
